name: Flevoland (Netherlands)

colors.symbolism: rapeseed fields and the gold of the iris

description: Blue upper half and green lower half divided by a horizontal yellow wavy band, with a white fleur-de-lis (yellow flag iris) in the upper hoist.
history: Adopted on 9 January 1986, the day Flevoland became a province after the reclamation of the IJsselmeerpolders. The design was created by Hindrik Veenstra and was the result of a public competition.

features.symbolism: blue above, green below — water (the IJsselmeer and former Zuiderzee) over reclaimed agricultural land

features.role: horizontal yellow wavy band along the centreline
features.symbolism: rapeseed-yellow band evoking the yellow flag iris that gave the province its name
the yellow flag iris (Dutch: gele lis), a wetland flower from which the name Flevoland is popularly derived; iconic of the IJsselmeer reclaimed lands
